paper_title: Geological Measurements and Solutions to the Faint Young Sun Problem

methods.description: This method involves analyzing volatiles trapped in fluid inclusions in ancient quartz to determine atmospheric pressure and composition in the distant past.

steps.description: Marty et al. [2013] analyzed volatiles trapped in fluid inclusions in 3.5 Ga hydrothermal quartz from the Pilbara, NW Australia.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze volatiles in ancient quartz samples

steps.description: In a 40Ar/36Ar versus N2/36Ar frame, these authors found that data from vacuum crushing experiments define a straight line representing mixing between a low 40Ar/36Ar, N2/36Ar end-member representing paleo-atmospheric noble gases dissolved in seawater and a hydrothermal component rich in crustal 40Ar and N.
steps.searchable_summary: perform isotopic ratio analysis on volatile data

steps.description: The extrapolated seawater N2/36Ar ratio was comparable to, or lower than, the modern value, leading Marty et al. [2013] to propose that the pN2 was ≤1.1 bar and possibly as low as 0.5 bar.
steps.searchable_summary: extrapolate seawater N2/36Ar ratio to estimate past atmospheric pressure

methods.description: This method uses fossil imprints of raindrops to set constraints on the barometric pressure in the distant past.

steps.description: Som et al. [2012] measured 2.7 Ga imprints of raindrops.
Conducted in parallel analogic experiments to conclude that the Archean atmospheric pressure could not have been higher than 2.3 bar and was probably below 1.3 bar.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze raindrop imprint data to estimate past atmospheric pressure

methods.method_name: Bubble Size Distribution Analysis in Basaltic Lava Flows
methods.description: This method involves analyzing the size distribution of gas bubbles in ancient basaltic lava flows to estimate past atmospheric pressure.

steps.description: Som et al. [2016] proposed an absolute barometric pressure of 0.23 ± 0.23 bar using the size distribution of gas bubbles in 2.5 Ga basaltic lava flows that solidified at sea level.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze gas bubble sizes in ancient lava flows

steps.description: They compared the bubble sizes for two different layers in the flow, at a given depth and at the flow’s surface, determined from morphological arguments.
steps.searchable_summary: compare bubble sizes at different depths to estimate atmospheric pressure
